homepage.heroSubtitle: Explore salaries, taxes, and cost of living across 30+ countries.
homepage.statsLine: 30+ Countries · 50+ Job Titles · Real Tax Data
homepage.topPayingCountriesDesc: Countries with the highest average tech salaries
homepage.popularJobsDesc: Explore salary ranges for trending tech roles
homepage.introP1: PayMapper helps developers, designers, and tech professionals understand how pay, taxes, and living costs compare across the world. Whether you're considering a job offer abroad, negotiating a raise, or planning a relocation, having reliable reference data in one place makes the decision easier.
homepage.introP2: We track salary ranges for more than 50 job titles across 30+ countries, covering entry-level, average, median, and senior compensation. Every country page also includes income tax brackets, social security contributions, and a cost of living breakdown — the three variables that determine what you actually keep each month.
homepage.introP3: All salaries are normalized to USD so you can compare countries side by side without mental math. Use the search bar above to look up a specific role, browse countries to see where your skills pay the most, or open the calculator to estimate your net take-home pay anywhere in the world.
homepage.featureCompareDesc: Line up 2–5 countries for any job and see gross pay, net pay, and monthly savings potential at a glance.
homepage.featureTaxDesc: Our net salary estimates use actual income tax brackets and social security rates, not flat assumptions.
homepage.featureCostDesc: A high salary in an expensive city isn’t always better. We show rent, groceries, transport, and utilities so you can judge purchasing power.
homepage.howToUseStep1Desc: Search for a job title or browse the categories to see a global ranking of average salaries for that role.
homepage.howToUseStep2Desc: Open any country to see the top-paying jobs, tax information, and cost of living index.
homepage.howToUseStep3Desc: Use the calculator to convert a gross offer into an estimated take-home amount based on real tax data.

jobs.title: Browse Jobs
jobs.subtitle: Explore salary data for tech jobs across the globe
jobs.noJobsHint: Try a different search term or category.
jobs.noSalaryData: No salary data is available for this job yet. Check back soon.
jobs.aboutRoleP1: Salaries for {jobTitle} positions vary significantly by country, driven by local demand for the skillset, the cost of living in major hiring hubs, prevailing tax rates, and the maturity of the local tech ecosystem. The figures on this page reflect averages across {count} countries where we track compensation data for this role.
jobs.aboutRoleP2: The highest-paying country in our dataset for {jobTitle} is {topCountry}, with an average of {topSalary} per year. The range between entry-level and senior compensation is typically where the largest gaps appear — experience, specialization, and company size all influence where an individual lands within a country’s band.
jobs.aboutRoleP3: When comparing offers across borders, keep in mind that gross salary is only part of the story. Effective tax rates, mandatory social security contributions, and the local cost of living can dramatically change how much of your paycheck you actually keep. Use our calculator or the salary detail pages to see net take-home estimates for any country.

countries.title: Browse Countries
countries.subtitle: Explore salary data and cost of living across {count} countries
countries.aboutCountryP1: {countryName} currently has salary data for {jobCount} tech and professional roles in our database. The average compensation across those roles is {avgSalary} per year, with a cost of living index of {costIndex} relative to New York City (100). This combination of pay and purchasing power is one of the most practical ways to evaluate whether a country is a good fit for your career and lifestyle.
countries.aboutCountryP2: Before accepting an offer in {countryName}, it’s worth looking at three numbers together: the gross salary, the effective tax rate at that income level, and the major monthly expenses like rent, groceries, and transport. Our tax tables include real income tax brackets and social security rates, and the cost of living breakdown shows typical monthly costs in the main cities.
countries.aboutCountryP3: Use the salary table below to see which roles pay best in {countryName}, or jump to any individual job for a full net salary estimate tailored to this country. Remember that averages hide variation: experience level, industry, company size, and city all influence where an individual offer lands within the range.

salary.title: {jobTitle} Salary in {countryName}
salary.annualDataInUsd: Annual salary data in USD for {year}
salary.taxDataNotAvailable: Tax data is not available for this country.
salary.costDataNotAvailable: Cost of living data is not available for this country.
salary.insightsP1: The average {jobTitle} in {countryName} earns around {avg} per year, with typical offers ranging from {min} at entry level to {max} for senior positions. These figures reflect gross compensation before tax — your actual take-home pay will depend on the effective tax rate and any social security contributions that apply at your income level.
salary.insightsP2: Several factors influence where an individual salary lands within this range. Experience is the biggest one: a candidate with five or more years of relevant work will typically earn significantly more than someone starting out. Company size and funding stage also matter; large multinationals and well-funded startups tend to pay above the local average, while smaller local businesses may pay below it. City matters too, with major tech hubs usually offering higher base salaries to offset steeper living costs.
salary.insightsP3: When evaluating an offer, compare the gross number against the cost of living data on this page. A salary that looks high on paper can quickly shrink once rent, transport, utilities, and groceries are accounted for. Conversely, a lower headline number in a cheaper country can leave you with more disposable income at the end of the month. Our net salary calculator can help you model these trade-offs precisely.

compare.title: Compare Salaries
compare.subtitle: Select a job and countries to see a side-by-side salary comparison.

calculator.title: Net Salary Calculator
calculator.subtitle: Enter your gross annual salary and select a country to estimate your take-home pay after taxes. Our calculator uses real tax bracket data to provide an accurate estimate.
calculator.enterSalaryPrompt: Enter your salary and select a country to see your estimated take-home pay.
calculator.invalidSalary: Please enter a valid salary amount.
calculator.taxNotAvailable: Tax data is not available for this country.
calculator.calcFailed: Failed to calculate. Please try again.
calculator.taxExplainer1: Our net salary calculations use real-world tax bracket data for each country. We estimate the effective tax rate by interpolating between known brackets (at $30K, $50K, $75K, $100K, and $150K income levels).
calculator.taxExplainer2: The calculation includes income tax and social security contributions (employee portion). Please note that actual take-home pay may vary based on your personal circumstances, deductions, filing status, and local/state taxes.
calculator.taxExplainer3: All salaries are displayed in USD for easy comparison. Currency conversion rates are approximate and may differ from current market rates.
calculator.disclaimer: Disclaimer: This calculator provides estimates for informational purposes only. It should not be used as a substitute for professional tax advice. Always consult a qualified tax professional for your specific situation.
calculator.whyNetTitle: Why net salary matters more than gross
calculator.whyNetP1: Job offers are almost always quoted in gross terms — the amount before income tax, social security, and other mandatory deductions are taken out. For cross-border comparisons this is misleading, because two countries can advertise identical gross salaries while delivering very different amounts into your bank account each month. A gross figure tells you what a company is willing to pay; a net figure tells you what you can actually spend, save, or send home.
calculator.whyNetP2: Effective tax rates on the same income can differ by more than 20 percentage points between countries. Add social security contributions, which range from a few percent to over 20% of gross pay, and the picture changes further. Our calculator applies the real bracketed tax logic for each country rather than a flat percentage, so a $100,000 offer in one country produces a realistically different net than the same number in another.
calculator.whyNetP3: When you have the net number, compare it against the cost of living in the country you are looking at. A lower net in a cheaper country can easily leave you with more monthly savings than a higher net in an expensive one. The country detail pages linked from each result give you rent, groceries, transport, and utility figures to make that final comparison.

map.title: Global Salary Map
map.subtitle: Explore salary data visually across the world. Select a job to see how pay varies by country.
map.selectJobPrompt: Select a job to see salary data on the map
map.aboutMapP1: The Global Salary Map is the fastest way to get a visual sense of how pay for a specific role varies around the world. Pick a job from the selector above and the map highlights each country we track, with the average compensation shown on hover. Darker shading indicates higher average salaries for that role.
map.aboutMapP2: Because countries differ dramatically in tax treatment and cost of living, a higher average salary does not always mean higher real purchasing power. If you are planning a move or comparing offers, use the country detail pages linked from the map to check tax brackets and living costs before drawing conclusions.
map.aboutMapP3: All salaries on the map are gross annual figures normalized to USD for comparability. Use the ranking list alongside the map when you want to see numeric differences between top-paying countries at a glance, or open a specific country to drill into the full salary, tax, and cost-of-living breakdown.

about.title: About PayMapper
about.intro: PayMapper is an independent salary research tool that helps tech professionals, employers, and career changers understand how compensation really compares across the world. We bring salary ranges, income tax rates, and cost of living data into a single place so you can make informed decisions about your career.
about.missionText: Salary information has historically been opaque, fragmented, and often limited to one country at a time. Our mission is to make cross-border compensation data accessible and easy to understand. We believe that when people have reliable reference points — not just a headline salary number, but also the taxes and living costs that shape it — they negotiate better, relocate more confidently, and build stronger careers.
about.whatWeTrackIntro: PayMapper currently covers more than 50 job titles across 30+ countries. For each role and country, we track:
about.whatWeTrackItem1: Gross annual salary ranges — minimum, average, median, and maximum figures
about.whatWeTrackItem2: Experience tiers — entry-level and senior compensation where available
about.whatWeTrackItem3: Income tax brackets and effective rates at multiple income levels
about.whatWeTrackItem5: Cost of living indices including rent, groceries, transport, and utilities
about.dataSourcesText: Our dataset is compiled from publicly available sources: national statistics offices, published tax codes, labor market reports, and aggregated salary surveys. Cost of living figures are benchmarked against widely used indices, with New York City set to 100 as a reference point. Because we normalize all salaries to USD for comparability, the figures you see reflect currency conversions that may differ slightly from live exchange rates on any given day.
about.methodologyText: Net salary estimates use real income tax brackets for each country, interpolated across several income levels (30K, 50K, 75K, 100K, and 150K USD) to produce an effective tax rate for any gross salary. We add the employee portion of social security contributions on top of income tax. The result is closer to reality than a flat percentage, but it is still an estimate — personal deductions, filing status, local or state taxes, and employer benefits can all move the final number.
about.accuracyText: We work hard to keep the data current and representative, but PayMapper should be treated as a research and planning tool, not a source of legal or financial advice. Salary ranges are aggregates and will not match every individual’s experience. Tax laws change regularly, and specific situations (dependents, bonuses, equity compensation, freelance income) are beyond the scope of a general calculator. Always verify important decisions with a qualified tax or relocation professional.
about.privacyText: PayMapper is free to use and funded by unobtrusive advertising. We do not sell personal data, we do not require an account, and we do not track individual users across sites. You can read the full details in our Privacy Policy and Terms of Service.
about.contactCtaText: We’re always improving the dataset and the site. If you’ve spotted an error, have a suggestion, or want to talk about a partnership, we’d love to hear from you.
about.exploreCtaText: Ready to dig into the data?

contact.title: Contact PayMapper
contact.intro: We’d love to hear from you. Whether you’ve found a data issue, have a suggestion, or want to ask a question, send us a message and we’ll do our best to get back to you within a few business days.
contact.emailText: The fastest way to reach us is by email. Please include as much detail as possible so we can help you quickly.
contact.reason1Text: Spotted a salary figure, tax rate, or cost of living number that looks wrong? Tell us which page and what you think the correct value should be, and we’ll investigate.
contact.reason2Text: If we’re missing a job title or country you’d find useful, let us know. We regularly expand the dataset based on reader demand.
contact.reason3Text: Have an idea for a new feature or a way to make the site easier to use? We read every message and prioritize ideas that benefit the most people.
contact.reason4Text: For media inquiries, research collaborations, or content partnerships, please include context about your organization and what you have in mind.
contact.reason5Text: For questions about how we handle data, our Privacy Policy, or our Terms of Service, please mention the relevant section in your message.
contact.responseText: We’re a small team, so please allow up to three business days for a reply. For data-correction requests, sharing a source link (official statistics, published salary survey, etc.) helps us verify and update the information much faster.
contact.beforeWritingText: Many common questions are already answered on our Frequently Asked Questions page — it’s worth a quick look before sending a message.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick answers to the questions we hear most often. If you don’t find what you’re looking for, please get in touch.
faq.a1: PayMapper is a free salary comparison tool for tech and professional roles. We aggregate gross salary ranges, income tax brackets, and cost of living data for more than 30 countries so you can compare compensation across borders in one place.
faq.q2: Where does your salary data come from?
faq.a2: Our figures are compiled from publicly available sources including national statistics offices, published tax codes, labor market reports, and aggregated salary surveys. We focus on sources that are updated regularly and widely cited by researchers and journalists.
faq.a3: We put significant effort into keeping the data representative and up to date, but all figures are aggregates and estimates. Individual offers will always vary based on experience, company, city, and negotiation. Treat PayMapper as a reference point, not a guarantee.
faq.q4: Why are all salaries shown in USD?
faq.a4: USD is used as a common denominator so you can compare countries directly without mental currency math. Conversions are based on representative exchange rates and may differ from live market rates on any given day. If you need to think in local currency, multiply by the current rate.
faq.q5: How are net salary estimates calculated?
faq.a5: We use real income tax brackets for each country and interpolate effective tax rates across several income levels (30K, 50K, 75K, 100K, and 150K USD). Employee social security contributions are added on top. The result is more realistic than a flat rate but does not account for personal deductions, filing status, or local taxes.
faq.q6: Do the salaries include bonuses, equity, or benefits?
faq.a6: No. All figures represent gross base salary only. Bonuses, stock options, RSUs, and non-cash benefits vary enormously by company and are not included in our aggregates. Remember to factor these in separately when comparing offers.
faq.q7: How often is the data updated?
faq.a7: We refresh the dataset periodically as new survey data and tax changes become available. Tax brackets are reviewed at least once per year to reflect legislative changes. You can always contact us if you notice stale information.
faq.q8: Why is my country or job title not listed?
faq.a8: We started with tech-focused roles and the countries with the best publicly available data. We expand the dataset regularly based on reader demand — if you’d like to see something added, please send us a message.
faq.q9: Does PayMapper offer an API or bulk data access?
faq.a9: Not at the moment. If you have a research, journalism, or commercial use case that would benefit from structured access, please reach out and tell us what you need.
faq.a10: Yes. The site is entirely free and funded by unobtrusive advertising. We do not sell personal data, require an account, or gate features behind a paywall.
faq.q11: Can I use PayMapper data in my article, presentation, or report?
faq.a11: For personal, educational, or non-commercial reference use, yes — a credit and link back to PayMapper is appreciated. For commercial republication or redistribution, please contact us first.
faq.q12: How does the cost of living index work?
faq.a12: We use New York City as a reference point with an index of 100. A country with a cost of living index of 60 is roughly 40% cheaper than NYC on a basket of common expenses like rent, groceries, transport, and utilities. This gives you a quick way to judge purchasing power alongside the salary number.
faq.q13: Do your tax calculations apply to freelancers and contractors?
faq.a13: Our calculator is designed for salaried employees. Self-employed, freelance, and contractor tax treatment is different in most countries and typically involves additional social security contributions or different brackets. Consult a local tax professional for your situation.
faq.q14: How do I report an error?
faq.a14: Please use the contact page and include the URL of the page with the issue, the specific value you believe is wrong, and a link to a source if you have one. Sourced corrections are investigated and applied much faster than unsourced ones.
faq.a15: PayMapper is an independent project built by a small team that cares about salary transparency. We are not affiliated with any recruiting firm, job board, or specific company.

metadata.defaultDescription: Compare developer and tech salaries across countries. Explore salary data, tax rates, and cost of living for software engineers, data scientists, and more.

metadata.countriesDescription: Explore salary data across 30+ countries. Compare average tech salaries, cost of living indices, and more.
metadata.searchDescription: Search for tech salaries by job title or country. Find detailed salary data, tax rates, and cost of living information.
metadata.calculatorDescription: Calculate your net salary after taxes in any country. Compare take-home pay across different countries and salary levels.

metadata.contactDescription: Get in touch with PayMapper. Report data issues, suggest new countries or roles, send feedback, or ask a question about our salary and tax data.
metadata.faqDescription: Answers to common questions about PayMapper salary data, methodology, tax calculations, cost of living index, and how we keep information up to date.
metadata.jobPageDescription: See {jobTitle} salaries worldwide. Compare pay across countries with detailed tax and cost of living breakdowns.
metadata.countryPageDescription: Explore tech salary data in {countryName}. See average salaries, top paying jobs, cost of living, and tax information.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Intro: PayMapper is designed to be privacy-friendly. We collect minimal information to provide and improve our Service:
privacy.section1Item1: Usage data: Pages visited, search queries, and interaction patterns (anonymized)
privacy.section1Item2: Technical data: Browser type, device type, and IP address (for security and analytics)
privacy.section1Item3: Cookies: Essential cookies for site functionality and optional analytics cookies
privacy.section2Intro: We use the collected information to:
privacy.section2Item2: Improve user experience and site performance
privacy.section2Item3: Analyze usage trends and popular content
privacy.section3Intro: We may use the following third-party services:
privacy.section3Item1: Google Analytics: For understanding how users interact with the Service
privacy.section3Item2: Google AdSense: For displaying relevant advertisements
privacy.section3Outro: These services may collect information in accordance with their own privacy policies. We encourage you to review their respective policies.
privacy.section4Text: We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties. We may share anonymized, aggregated data for analytical purposes.
privacy.section5Text: We retain usage data for a reasonable period necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this policy. You may request deletion of any personal data by contacting us.
privacy.section6Intro: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:
privacy.section6Item1: Access the personal data we hold about you
privacy.section6Item2: Request correction or deletion of your data
privacy.section6Item4: Request a copy of your data in a portable format
privacy.section7Text: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data. However, no method of transmission over the Internet is 100% secure.
privacy.section8Text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify users of any material changes by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page.
privacy.section9Text: For privacy-related inquiries, please contact us at

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1Text: By accessing and using PayMapper ("the Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use the Service.
terms.section2Text: PayMapper provides salary comparison data, cost of living information, and tax estimates for informational purposes only. The data presented is aggregated from public sources and should not be considered financial, legal, or tax advice.
terms.section3Text: While we strive to keep information accurate and up to date, we make no warranties or representations about the accuracy, completeness, or reliability of any data displayed on the platform. Salary figures, tax rates, and cost of living data are estimates and may vary based on individual circumstances.
terms.section4Intro: You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree not to:
terms.section4Item1: Scrape or collect data from the Service in an automated manner without prior consent
terms.section4Item2: Attempt to interfere with or disrupt the Service or its infrastructure
terms.section4Item3: Use the Service for any commercial purpose without authorization
terms.section4Item4: Reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works from the Service content
terms.section5Text: All content, design, and data compilations on PayMapper are the property of PayMapper and are protected by applicable intellectual property laws. Individual data points sourced from public datasets retain their original licensing terms.
terms.section6Text: PayMapper shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the Service. This includes, but is not limited to, any decisions made based on the salary, tax, or cost of living data provided.
terms.section7Text: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Changes will be effective immediately upon posting. Your continued use of the Service constitutes acceptance of the modified Terms.
terms.section8Text: If you have questions about these Terms, please contact us at
